goal: Generate concise quote-card and source-caption text variants that fit the archival, scholarly tone of the documentary series and can drop into the existing reusable SVG templates.

visual_requirements.tone: scholarly, sober, cinematic, readable on screen
visual_requirements.color_notes: designed to sit on dark parchment, aged gold headings, restrained red accents
visual_requirements.composition_notes: short lines, fast readability, clear hierarchy between quotation, source, and date
must_include: one slightly more dramatic but credible option

text_inputs.primary_text: Replace with the exact quotation or thesis line.
text_inputs.secondary_text: Replace with the speaker, source title, or source caption line.
dates: Replace with date or period if needed
names: Replace with speaker, author, institution, or location
citations: Replace with exact citation text if needed

review_checks: Readable in under two seconds for major lines
Works inside the existing reusable card templates
Still sounds like the same documentary voice
destination_after_approval: Approved copy can be pasted into reusable files under visual-library/02-series-kits/episode-neutral/ or stored as episode-specific text drafts outside the reusable kit.
run_notes: Best paired with text-population-template.md. After generation, keep only the most legible 1 to 3 options and log the chosen variant in 05-ai-automation/runs/.
